skill.name: uniswap
skill.description: Build Python workflows for Uniswap using Ape, ape-tokens, and the uniswap-sdk package.
skill.summary: Create Ape-based Uniswap scripts for pricing, routing, indexing, and swaps.
skill.author: ApeWorX

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and blockchain terminology in SKILL.md. The real risks are external documentation fetching and guidance for live Uniswap trading, which can move user assets if used without confirmation.

medium_findings.title: Live Trading Workflow Can Move User Assets
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed for Uniswap trading and acknowledges that trades are risky. It instructs agents to query prices and ask the user before trading, but misuse could still result in unwanted swaps or financial loss.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text directly discusses performing trades and mitigation steps. This is not malicious, but the workflow has clear financial impact.

low_findings.title: Documentation Fetching Requires Network Access
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to fetch Uniswap SDK, Ape, and ape-tokens documentation from GitHub and ApeWorX documentation sites. These are legitimate documentation URLs, and no evidence of data exfiltration was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URLs are visible and point to project documentation. The file does not instruct sending local files, secrets, or account data to those URLs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged inline Markdown code spans as shell or Ruby backtick execution. SKILL.md is prose documentation and contains no executable script blocks or shell invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown text with inline code formatting. No command interpreter, script file, or dynamic execution context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Blockchain Terms Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography findings appear to come from blockchain and trading terminology, not from use of weak algorithms. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or custom cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe Uniswap, Ape accounts, and documentation links. They do not name or invoke cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Token Names Flagged as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding points to token symbols and routing guidance. No evidence found of host enumeration, process listing, network scanning, or environment inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line lists WETH, USDC, and USDT as possible route intermediates. Those are blockchain token symbols, not system reconnaissance commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Documentation Fetch Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks agents to fetch current documentation before producing code. This is a legitimate accuracy measure, but it requires network access and should be limited to the listed documentation sources.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Network fetching is explicitly requested in the file. The destinations are documentation URLs, which lowers the security concern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Live Swap Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill covers actions such as route searches and trades on Uniswap. Any generated workflow should require explicit user approval before signing or broadcasting transactions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly lists trades as an intended action and includes trade-risk guidance. This confirms the financial-risk pattern without indicating malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Uniswap automation is difficult because routing, token indexing, and account setup vary by network. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use Ape and uniswap-sdk with current documentation.
actual_capabilities: Guides Python code generation for uniswap-sdk workflows.
Prompts agents to fetch current Ape and SDK documentation before coding.
Helps identify network, token, and routing inputs before implementation.
Explains pair indexing with uni.index or uni.install for Silverback usage.
Encourages price checks and small test swaps before larger trades.
Describes using liquid route intermediates such as wrapped native tokens and stablecoins.
limitations: Does not include executable helper scripts or packaged Python modules.
Requires configured Ape accounts and installed token lists.
Depends on external documentation being reachable and current.
Does not remove the financial risk of live blockchain trading.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Swap Script
use_cases.description: Create a Python plan for indexing pairs, checking a price, and preparing a cautious Uniswap swap.

use_cases.title: Plan Route Discovery
use_cases.description: Compare token route inputs across networks and choose likely liquid intermediates before writing code.

use_cases.title: Design a Silverback Integration
use_cases.description: Outline how a bot should use uniswap-sdk indexing and routing instead of custom Uniswap logic.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Setup Requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Uniswap skill to list the Ape, ape-tokens, account, and network details needed before I write a Uniswap script.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Price Query Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Uniswap skill to plan a Python workflow that indexes pairs and checks the price between two tokens on my chosen network.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Cautious Swap Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Uniswap skill to draft a swap implementation plan that checks price, estimates a route, and asks for confirmation before any transaction.

prompt_templates.title: Design Silverback Routing Logic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Uniswap skill to design a Silverback bot workflow that keeps Uniswap pair data indexed and routes swaps through liquid intermediates.

output_examples.input: Plan a read-only Uniswap price check for WETH and USDC on Ethereum.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan that fetches current Ape and uniswap-sdk documentation, confirms account and tokenlist prerequisites, indexes relevant pairs, and reports a price query workflow.

output_examples.input: Help me prepare a safe swap workflow for a new token pair.
output_examples.output: A staged workflow that identifies network details, checks liquidity, estimates routes, requests explicit user approval, and recommends a small test swap before a larger trade.

output_examples.input: Design a Silverback bot that uses Uniswap routes.
output_examples.output: An architecture outline covering documentation checks, pair indexing, route refresh behavior, liquidity-aware intermediates, and safeguards before sending transactions.

best_practices: Fetch current Ape, ape-tokens, and uniswap-sdk documentation before generating implementation details.
Use read-only price and route checks before asking the user to approve any transaction.
Start new or large trade workflows with a small test swap.
anti_patterns: Do not sign or broadcast swaps without explicit user confirmation.
Do not assume token addresses or route intermediates are the same on every network.
Do not replace uniswap-sdk routing with custom swap logic unless the user requires it.

faq.question: Does this skill execute Uniswap trades by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for generating Python workflows. Any live trade still requires configured tools and user approval.

faq.question: Why does the skill request web fetching?
faq.answer: It asks agents to read current Ape and uniswap-sdk documentation because these tools can change over time.

faq.question: Which tools does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Ape, ape-tokens, uniswap-sdk, and Silverback bot integration.

faq.question: Can it help with token route selection?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides the agent to consider wrapped native tokens and liquid stablecoins as possible route intermediates.

faq.question: Is it safe for mainnet trading?
faq.answer: Mainnet trading always carries financial risk. The skill recommends price checks, confirmation, and small test swaps.

faq.question: Does it include complete source code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown instruction file that helps an AI assistant generate appropriate code.
